messages.content: "Based on the above variation in results for “Enantiomeric content by HPLC test” unit of measurement for LOD and LOQ found as ppm instead of %, however reporting result no abnormality except unit of measurement  w r t Specification".  give the constructive sentence
The test results for "Enantiomeric content by HPLC test" exhibit variations, but no abnormalities were observed except for the unit of measurement, which was reported in ppm instead of %.

The test results for “Enantiomeric content by HPLC test” exhibit variations, but no abnormalities were observed except for the unit of measurement, which was reported in "ppm" instead of  "%" for the limit of Detection (LOD ) and limit of Quantification (LOQ) with respect to testing procedure .

The test results for "Enantiomeric content by HPLC test" displayed variations, with no abnormalities detected, except for the unit of measurement. The Limit of Detection (LOD) and Limit of Quantification (LOQ) were reported in "ppm" instead of "%" according to the testing procedure.

"Verified the specification and test plan in the LIMS master and observed that Enantiomer content (% w/w, by HPLC) test, Limit Of Detection (LOD) and Limit Of Quantification (LOQ) Text For COA given as  BDL (LOD: 0.004 ppm) and BQL (LOQ: 0.012 ppm) Instead of BDL (LOD: 0.004%) and BQL (LOQ: 0.012%)".    give the constructive sentence for this

Upon verification of the specification and test plan in the LIMS master, it was observed that for the Enantiomer content (% w/w, by HPLC) test, the Limit of Detection (LOD) and Limit of Quantification (LOQ) text provided in the Certificate of Analysis (COA) indicated "BDL (LOD: 0.004 ppm)" and "BQL (LOQ: 0.012 ppm)" instead of "BDL (LOD: 0.004%)" and "BQL (LOQ: 0.012%)" as specified.
Verified the other specification in LIMS test plan against STP, found no abnormality.
Upon verification of the other specifications in the LIMS test plan against the Standard Testing Procedure (STP), no abnormalities were detected.
The LIMS master team (H.Madhusudhana Rao and B.Jagannadha Naidu) prepared & reviewed the test plan XXXX with STP (STP/FPR/TOC/003/00) and LIMS revision No.:0.

The LIMS master team, consisting of H. Madhusudhana Rao and B. Jagannadha Naidu, prepared and reviewed the test plan XXXX in accordance with the Standard Testing Procedure (STP/FPR/TOC/003/00). The LIMS revision number is 0.

"The discrepancy was discussed with the LIMS master team (H.Madhusudhana Rao and B.Jagannadha Naidu) and conformed that, during the preparation of LIMS test plan, LIMS initiator by oversight Limit of Detection (LOD) and Limit of Quantification (LOQ) in the Text for COA given as “BDL (LOD: 0.004 ppm)” and “BQL (LOQ: 0.012 ppm)” instead of “BDL (LOD: 0.004%)” and “BQL (LOQ: 0.012%)” for the Enantiomer content (% w/w, by HPLC) test".

The discrepancy was addressed by discussing it with the LIMS master team, comprising H. Madhusudhana Rao and B. Jagannadha Naidu. It was confirmed that during the preparation of the LIMS test plan, there was an oversight where the Limit of Detection (LOD) and Limit of Quantification (LOQ) in the Text for Certificate of Analysis (COA) were specified as "BDL (LOD: 0.004 ppm)" and "BQL (LOQ: 0.012 ppm)" instead of "BDL (LOD: 0.004%)" and "BQL (LOQ: 0.012%)" for the Enantiomer content (% w/w, by HPLC) test.

"LIMS team verified the subject product LIMS master test plan of concern STP STP/FPR/TOC/003/00  and found in the Text for Certificate of Analysis (COA) were specified as ppm for the Enantiomeric content by HPLC".

The LIMS team conducted a verification of the subject product's LIMS master test plan, specifically the STP STP/FPR/TOC/003/00, and identified that the Text for Certificate of Analysis (COA) specified the unit as ppm for the Enantiomeric content by HPLC test.
"Then LIMS master team (H Madhusudhana Rao & B Jagannadha Naidu) prepared the test plan on XXXX   with STP (STP/FPR/TOC/003/00) and LIMS Revision No.:0".

Following the identification of the discrepancy, the LIMS master team, composed of H. Madhusudhana Rao and B. Jagannadha Naidu, proceeded to prepare the test plan for XXXX in alignment with the STP (STP/FPR/TOC/003/00), with the LIMS revision number assigned as 0.

LIMS Master team corrected the Enantiomer content (% w/w, by HPLC) test by modified the unit of measurement from "ppm" to "% " in the Test for COA  for Limit of detection (LOD) and Limit of Quantification (LOQ) for subject Master test plan (LIMS Version No: 00 and STP/FPR/TOC/003/00) on XXXXX.

The LIMS master team rectified the Enantiomer content (% w/w, by HPLC) test by modifying the unit of measurement from "ppm" to "%" in the Test for Certificate of Analysis (COA) for both the Limit of Detection (LOD) and Limit of Quantification (LOQ). This correction was made for the subject Master test plan, which corresponds to LIMS Version No: 00 and STP/FPR/TOC/003/00, on XXXXX.
"After correction of LIMS test plan Tofacitinib citrate (STP/FPR/TOC/003/00) subject batches ((25524516, 25524517, and 25524952) COA s were recertified".
After rectifying the LIMS test plan for Tofacitinib citrate (STP/FPR/TOC/003/00), the Certificate of Analysis (COA) for subject batches (25524516, 25524517, and 25524952) were re-certified.

"Verified the previous LIRs (OOS/OOT/Invalid/QIA) from last three months 26.06.2023 to 26.09.2023, found no  LIR with respect to “typo error” for the analyst (H Madhu) during LIMS master test plan, hence no potential trend identified (SOP criteria, 2 or more per quarter, SOP:000460758). Refer trend PR # XXX".

Upon verification of the previous Laboratory Investigation Reports (LIRs) from the period of 26.06.2023 to 26.09.2023, it was determined that there were no LIRs related to "typo error" by the analyst (H Madhu) during the LIMS master test plan. Consequently, no potential trend was identified based on the SOP criteria, which states that at least 2 or more occurrences per quarter are required (refer to SOP:000460758). Please refer to trend PR # XXX for further details.
"Verified the previous approved batches found total 21 batches, 10 batches analyzed as per STP/FOR/TOC/001/00 and 12 batches were analyzed as per STP/FPR/TOC/002/00"

Upon verification of the previous approved batches, it was found that a total of 21 batches were reviewed. Out of these, 10 batches were analyzed in accordance with the STP/FOR/TOC/001/00, while 12 batches were analyzed following the STP/FPR/TOC/002/00.

"To assess the impact, an evaluation was conducted on the other STPs test plans of Tofacitinib citrate. It was observed that the same discrepancy existed in the STP/FPR/TOC/001, STP/FPR/TOC/001(STB), STP/FPR/TOC/002, and STP/FPR/TOC/003 test plans, where enantiomer content units were denoted as ‘ppm’ instead of ‘%’. However, no abnormality was found in the STP/FPR/TOC/003 (STB) test plan".

To assess the impact, an evaluation was conducted on the other Standard Testing Procedures (STPs) test plans for Tofacitinib citrate. The evaluation revealed that the same discrepancy regarding the unit of measurement for enantiomer content (denoted as "ppm" instead of "%") was present in the following test plans: STP/FPR/TOC/001, STP/FPR/TOC/001(STB), STP/FPR/TOC/002, and STP/FPR/TOC/003. However, it is important to note that no abnormality was identified in the STP/FPR/TOC/003 (STB) test plan.
"This includes modifying the Enantiomer content by HPLC test and the test plans (STP/FPR/TOC/001, STP/FPR/TOC/001(STB) and STP/FPR/TOC/002 ) in accordance with the respective STP requirements"

The necessary modifications were made, including the adjustment of the Enantiomer content by HPLC test and the test plans for STP/FPR/TOC/001, STP/FPR/TOC/001(STB), and STP/FPR/TOC/002, ensuring compliance with the specific requirements outlined in each respective STP.

"Based on review of available documents and discussion with LIMS team, the reason for incident is due to  inadvertent typo error by the LIMS master test plan i.e. unit of measurement given in Test for: Certification of analysis (COA) as ppm instead of % for  Enantiomeric content by HPLC test".

Based on the review of available documents and discussions with the LIMS team, it has been determined that the incident occurred due to an inadvertent typo error in the LIMS master test plan. Specifically, the unit of measurement for the Enantiomeric content by HPLC test was incorrectly stated as "ppm" instead of "%" in the Test for Certification of Analysis (COA).
